evals.prompt: How do I test a standalone Angular 17 component with a signal input?
evals.expected_output: Import the component directly in TestBed.configureTestingModule({ imports: [UserCardComponent] }) — no module declaration needed for standalone. Set signal inputs with fixture.componentRef.setInput('user', mockUser). Always call fixture.detectChanges() after setting inputs or changing state to trigger template updates. Then query the DOM with fixture.nativeElement.

evals.prompt: How do I test an Angular service that makes HTTP calls?
evals.expected_output: Use provideHttpClientTesting() in the test providers. Inject HttpTestingController. After calling the service method, use httpTesting.expectOne('/api/url') to get the pending request, then call req.flush(mockData) to respond. Always call httpTesting.verify() in afterEach to assert no outstanding requests were left unhandled.

evals.prompt: How do I test a functional Angular route guard?
evals.expected_output: Use TestBed.runInInjectionContext(() => authGuard(route, state)) to call a functional guard — it needs an injection context since it uses inject() internally. Provide mock values via { provide: AuthStore, useValue: { isAuthenticated: signal(false) } }. The result is either true or a UrlTree — check for UrlTree to verify the redirect.
